#====================================================================================================
# START - Testing Protocol - DO NOT EDIT OR REMOVE THIS SECTION
#====================================================================================================

# THIS SECTION CONTAINS CRITICAL TESTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR BOTH AGENTS
# BOTH MAIN_AGENT AND TESTING_AGENT MUST PRESERVE THIS ENTIRE BLOCK

# Communication Protocol:
# If the `testing_agent` is available, main agent should delegate all testing tasks to it.
#
# You have access to a file called `test_result.md`. This file contains the complete testing state
# and history, and is the primary means of communication between main and the testing agent.
#
# Main and testing agents must follow this exact format to maintain testing data. 
# The testing data must be entered in yaml format Below is the data structure:
# 
## user_problem_statement: {problem_statement}
## backend:
##   - task: "Task name"
##     implemented: true
##     working: true  # or false or "NA"
##     file: "file_path.py"
##     stuck_count: 0
##     priority: "high"  # or "medium" or "low"
##     needs_retesting: false
##     status_history:
##         -working: true  # or false or "NA"
##         -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##         -comment: "Detailed comment about status"
##
## frontend:
##   - task: "Task name"
##     implemented: true
##     working: true  # or false or "NA"
##     file: "file_path.js"
##     stuck_count: 0
##     priority: "high"  # or "medium" or "low"
##     needs_retesting: false
##     status_history:
##         -working: true  # or false or "NA"
##         -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##         -comment: "Detailed comment about status"
##
## metadata:
##   created_by: "main_agent"
##   version: "1.0"
##   test_sequence: 0
##   run_ui: false
##
## test_plan:
##   current_focus:
##     - "Task name 1"
##     - "Task name 2"
##   stuck_tasks:
##     - "Task name with persistent issues"
##   test_all: false
##   test_priority: "high_first"  # or "sequential" or "stuck_first"
##
## agent_communication:
##     -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##     -message: "Communication message between agents"

# Protocol Guidelines for Main agent
#
# 1. Update Test Result File Before Testing:
#    - Main agent must always update the `test_result.md` file before calling the testing agent
#    - Add implementation details to the status_history
#    - Set `needs_retesting` to true for tasks that need testing
#    - Update the `test_plan` section to guide testing priorities
#    - Add a message to `agent_communication` explaining what you've done
#
# 2. Incorporate User Feedback:
#    - When a user provides feedback that something is or isn't working, add this information to the relevant task's status_history
#    - Update the working status based on user feedback
#    - If a user reports an issue with a task that was marked as working, increment the stuck_count
#    - Whenever user reports issue in the app, if we have testing agent and task_result.md file so find the appropriate task for that and append in status_history of that task to contain the user concern and problem as well 
#
# 3. Track Stuck Tasks:
#    - Monitor which tasks have high stuck_count values or where you are fixing same issue again and again, analyze that when you read task_result.md
#    - For persistent issues, use websearch tool to find solutions
#    - Pay special attention to tasks in the stuck_tasks list
#    - When you fix an issue with a stuck task, don't reset the stuck_count until the testing agent confirms it's working
#
# 4. Provide Context to Testing Agent:
#    - When calling the testing agent, provide clear instructions about:
#      - Which tasks need testing (reference the test_plan)
#      - Any authentication details or configuration needed
#      - Specific test scenarios to focus on
#      - Any known issues or edge cases to verify
#
# 5. Call the testing agent with specific instructions referring to test_result.md
#
# IMPORTANT: Main agent must ALWAYS update test_result.md BEFORE calling the testing agent, as it relies on this file to understand what to test next.

#====================================================================================================
# END - Testing Protocol - DO NOT EDIT OR REMOVE THIS SECTION
#====================================================================================================



#====================================================================================================
# Testing Data - Main Agent and testing sub agent both should log testing data below this section
#====================================================================================================

          comment: "RE-TEST PASS (2026-05-08, /app/retest_s4_s8.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api) — 24/24 assertions PASS. The previously missing `_log_xp_to_charts(source='challenge_complete', focus_area='mindset')` call is now wired at server.py L2650-2652 immediately after `_bump_streak_for_xp`. Verified end-to-end:\n\n  ✅ S4 fresh gmail.com user → POST /challenge/accept 200 → POST /challenge/complete {completed:true, difficulty:'easy', rating:5} → response.awarded_xp=30, profile.total_xp delta=+30, /stats/weekly today.xp=30 (was 0 before fix), /stats/by-area.mindset=30, db.task_logs row with _source='challenge_complete', xp_awarded=30, focus_area='mindset' PRESENT.\n\n  ✅ S8 fresh gmail.com user, same day: focus/session(25min completed, locked=0)→xp_delta=+5; challenge/accept→200; challenge/complete(easy)→awarded_xp=30; confidence/complete(track:'social')→xp_awarded=15; /stats/weekly today.xp == EXACTLY 50 (5+30+15); /stats/by-area.mindset == EXACTLY 50. Aggregation now sums correctly across all three XP-grant paths.\n\n  No regressions; bug fix verified; task marked working."
        - working: false
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PARTIAL PASS — 43/54 assertions in /app/backend_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). The new `_log_xp_to_charts` helper is correctly wired for 4 of the 5 XP-grant paths, but ONE endpoint is missing the call: regular /challenge/complete. Detailed findings:\n\n  ✅ S3 Focus session — `_log_xp_to_charts(source='focus_session', focus_area='mindset')` fires from /focus/session L7287. /stats/weekly today.xp went 0→5 after 25-min completed session. by-area.mindset=5. task_log row with _source='focus_session' xp_awarded=5 confirmed via direct Mongo read.\n\n  ✅ S5 Challenge late-answer — `_log_xp_to_charts(source='challenge_late', focus_area='mindset')` fires from /challenge/past/{id}/answer L2768. Seeded an auto_uncompleted=true row directly in db.challenge_completions, then POST /challenge/past/{id}/answer with completed=true → awarded_xp=30, today.xp=30, _source='challenge_late' row present.\n\n  ✅ S6 Confidence track — `_log_xp_to_charts(source='confidence_track', focus_area='mindset')` fires from /confidence/complete L6043. POST /confidence/complete {track:'social'} → ok=true, xp_awarded=15, today.xp=15, _source='confidence_track' row present with xp_awarded=15.\n\n  ✅ S7 Gift XP correctly NOT logged into task_logs — admin gift of 50 XP increments today.gifted_xp by 50 but today.xp is unchanged AND zero task_log rows have any _source field for that user. profile.total_xp moved by 50.\n\n  ✅ S9 Regression — regular task complete still works AND does NOT create a duplicate _source row. Default 'Morning reflection' task complete → xp_awarded=15, today.xp ≥15, NO _source rows created (as designed — the regular task path writes a normal task_log row at L1579 without the _source tag), regular task_log row present.\n\n  ✅ S10 Cleanup — /profile, /stats/daily, /stats/weekly, /stats/monthly, /stats/by-area all 200 OK.\n\n  ❌ **CRITICAL BUG — S4 challenge_complete is missing _log_xp_to_charts**: /challenge/complete (server.py L2607-2661) does NOT call `_log_xp_to_charts`. Only the LATE-answer path (L2768) and the streak-bump call (L2647) fire after challenge XP is awarded. Verified by direct Mongo read: after POST /challenge/accept then POST /challenge/complete {completed:true,difficulty:'easy'} → awarded_xp=30 + profile.total_xp=30, BUT db.task_logs.find({user_id, _source:'challenge_complete'}) returns 0 rows AND /stats/weekly today.xp=0 (NOT 30 as the spec requires). FIX: add `await _log_xp_to_charts(user_id, awarded_xp, source='challenge_complete', focus_area='mindset')` immediately after `await _bump_streak_for_xp(user_id)` at server.py L2647.\n\n  ❌ S8 Aggregation correctness fails BECAUSE OF S4: today's xp aggregated from focus(5) + challenge(30) + step(30) should be 65. Test got 35 because the challenge 30 XP did not appear on the chart — direct downstream consequence of the S4 bug. Once S4 is fixed, S8 will pass automatically.\n\n  ⚠️ S1.5/S1.8 — TEST EXPECTATION (NOT BACKEND BUG): the spec said 'goal completion → total_xp 0→100' but the actual capped value for non-admin user with unit='days' is 30 (per GOAL_XP_CAPS in server.py L815). Furthermore, completing a unit='days' goal in ONE tick with target_value=1 ALSO awards step XP (30) on top of the completion bonus (30, capped from 100), so total_xp=60 — this is correct chained behaviour. The chart correctly shows 60 with both _source='goal_step' AND _source='goal_complete' rows present. NO BACKEND ACTION needed; spec assertion 'total_xp == awarded_xp' was naive.\n\n  ⚠️ S2 daily-step (3 ticks) is impossible to test as written — backend (correctly!) enforces a CYCLE LOCKOUT on unit='days' goals: after the 1st tick, /goals/{id}/progress with current_value=2 returns 429 'cycle_locked' until the next day cycle (next UTC midnight). The backend prevents same-day double-ticks of daily goals — this is intentional from a previously-shipped feature. So in this run only the 1st tick succeeded → 1 step row with _source='goal_step' xp_awarded=30, focus_area='fitness' was confirmed. The chart-logging machinery for goal_step is verified working; the spec's '3 ticks in one day' scenario simply can't happen.\n\n  ✅ S1 partial — _source='goal_complete' row WAS created on goal completion with focus_area='social' xp_awarded=30 (the capped completion bonus). by-area.social=60 (step+complete). The chart-logging path for goal_complete is working.\n\n  Summary: 4 of 5 _log_xp_to_charts call sites are wired correctly (goal_step, goal_complete, focus_session, challenge_late, confidence_track). The MISSING call site is in /challenge/complete (regular completion, not the late-answer path). One-line fix needed."

(1) Admin gift bumps streak: registered fresh gmail.com user → GET /profile shows current_streak=0 and last_active_date=null; admin (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com) POST /admin/gift/xp {user_id, amount:25, message:'test'} → 200 with body.ok=true; recipient GET /profile → current_streak=1, last_active_date='2026-05-08' (today UTC), total_xp grew exactly 0→25. (2) /stats/weekly: days length=7, days[6].date=='2026-05-08', xp=0, gifted_xp=25 (yellow stacked bar). /stats/monthly: days length=30, days[29].date=='2026-05-08' with gifted_xp=25. (3) Same-day idempotency: 2nd /admin/gift/xp same recipient → current_streak STILL 1 (not 2) — `update_streak()` short-circuits via `last == today` at server.py L701. (4) Focus session bump: fresh user POST /focus/session {planned_minutes:25, actual_seconds:1500, completed:true, locked_app_seconds:0, backgrounded_seconds:0, committed_app_count:3} → 200 with xp_delta=+5, reason='focus_complete'; subsequent /profile shows current_streak=1 (server.py L7204 `_bump_streak_for_xp` after positive delta). (5) Challenge complete bump: fresh user POST /challenge/accept → 200; POST /challenge/complete {completed:true, difficulty:'easy', rating:5, how_text, experience_text} → 200 with awarded_xp=30; /profile shows current_streak=1 (server.py L2629). (6) Streak cap @5000 (DIRECT DB SEED): registered fresh user, set profile.current_streak=5000, longest_streak=5000, last_active_date=yesterday via motor → 1 admin gift triggered _bump_streak_for_xp → update_streak computed new=5001 then HARD-CAPPED at 5000 (server.py L723-727); /profile reads current_streak=5000 (NOT 5001) AND db.profile.longest_streak=5000 (cap held on both fields). last_active_date == today UTC. (7) Regression — all common endpoints 200: /profile, /stats/daily, /stats/weekly, /stats/monthly, /stats/by-area, /library/ratings (recipient), /library/catalog (admin). No bugs found."

  - task: "Library+ pricing & purchases endpoints"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 86/86 assertions in /app/library_pricing_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Endpoints under test: GET /api/library/pricing, POST /api/library/pricing/{app_id}, POST /api/library/pricing/{app_id}/discount, POST /api/library/purchase/{app_id} (server.py L7240-7381). (S1) Anonymous (X-Anonymous-Id only, no Bearer) GET /library/pricing → 200 with response.pricing containing all 4 apps {sleep, challenges, spot, confidence} and response.currencies length=10 == [USD, EUR, GBP, AUD, CAD, JPY, INR, RON, CHF, BRL]. For an app with no doc / price=0 the default shape is exactly {price:0, currency:'USD', purchase_url:'', discount_percent:0, discount_active:false, effective_price:0, is_free:true, purchased:false}. (S2) Non-admin user POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99} → 403 with detail 'Creator only.'. (S3) Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:'https://ko-fi.com/example'} → 200 with body.pricing={price:9.99, currency:'USD', is_free:false, effective_price:9.99, purchase_url:'https://ko-fi.com/example', discount_active:false}. (S4) Invalid currency 'XYZ' → 400. (S5) Invalid purchase_url 'ftp://bad' → 400. (S6) Negative price -1 → 400. (S7) Invalid app_id 'foo' → 400. (S7b) Sleep price still 9.99 after rejected requests (no partial writes). (S8) Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep/discount {percent:50, duration_value:7, duration_unit:'days'} → 200 with discount_active=true, discount_percent=50, discount_starts_at and discount_ends_at non-null ISO timestamps, ends_at exactly 7.000 days ahead of now, effective_price=5.0 (within ±0.01 of expected 4.995 — backend rounds via `round(max(0, eff), 2)` at L7224 so 9.99*0.5=4.995 → 5.0). (S9) Admin POST same /discount {percent:0} → 200 clears: discount_active=false, effective_price=9.99 (full price restored). (S10) duration_value:0 → 400. (S11) percent:120 → 400. (S12) duration_unit:'years' → 400 (only days|weeks|months allowed). (S13) Non-admin POST /discount → 403. (S14) Fresh user A registered (gmail.com): a) GET /library/pricing → A.sleep.purchased=false, A.sleep.is_free=false; b) POST /library/purchase/sleep → 200 {saved:true, already_owned:false, is_free:false}; c) Re-POST → {saved:true, already_owned:true, is_free:false} (idempotent); d) GET /library/pricing → A.sleep.purchased=true. (S15) A POST /library/purchase/challenges (free, no doc) → 200 {saved:false, already_owned:true, is_free:true} — backend short-circuits free apps without inserting a library_purchases row (server.py L7368). Verified via subsequent GET: challenges.purchased=false (no row was inserted). (S16) POST /library/purchase/foo → 400 invalid app_id. (S17) Anonymous flow (X-Anonymous-Id only, no JWT): a) GET /library/pricing → 200, sleep.purchased=false initially; b) POST /library/purchase/sleep → 200 saved:true; c) Subsequent GET with same X-Anonymous-Id → sleep.purchased=true; d) Different X-Anonymous-Id → sleep.purchased=false (correct device isolation per anon_id). (S18) Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:0, currency:'USD'} → 200 is_free=true; A's GET still shows A.sleep.purchased=true (library_purchases row persists across price changes) AND A.sleep.is_free=true. (S19) Regression — GET /library/ratings still works for both admin and a freshly-registered user; both return ratings dict with all 4 apps. (S20) GET /library/pricing returns pricing for all 4 apps including challenges/spot/confidence with is_free=true by default. Edge cases verified: rounding (effective_price 4.995→5.0 within tolerance), idempotency (re-POST purchase returns already_owned=true), anonymous device isolation (different X-Anonymous-Id values see independent purchase state), persistence across pricing changes (purchased stays true even when admin sets price=0). No bugs found."

  - task: "Library+ Mini-App Ratings (GET/POST /api/library/ratings)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 66/66 assertions in /app/library_ratings_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). (1) GET /api/library/ratings — fresh gmail.com user (rate*@gmail.com) → 200 with response shape {ratings:{sleep:{...}, challenges:{...}, spot:{...}, confidence:{...}}}. Each app's stats has EXACTLY 3 keys {average:float|int, count:int, user_rating:int|null}. user_rating is null for fresh user across ALL 4 apps. NOTE on aggregate counts: the spec said 'fresh user with no prior ratings, ALL apps return {average:0, count:0, user_rating:null} initially' but on this live DB count is GLOBAL (not per-user) — there were already prior raters in db.library_ratings (sleep had count=2 at run start). user_rating=null IS satisfied for the fresh user (correct). The implementation aggregates across all users (server.py L6823: `db.library_ratings.find({})` with NO user filter), which is correct for a public 'community average' that everyone sees the same. (2) POST /api/library/ratings — Scenario A: {app_id:'sleep', stars:4} as fresh user → 200 with saved=true, app_id='sleep', stats.user_rating=4 (sleep avg recomputed correctly). Scenario B (update): {sleep, 5} → 200, stats.user_rating=5, count UNCHANGED from A (upsert semantics: same row updated, not inserted) — verified via follow-up GET sleep.count identical. Scenario C (different app): {challenges, 3} → 200, stats.user_rating=3; follow-up GET shows sleep.user_rating still 5, challenges.user_rating=3, spot/confidence still null. Scenario D (multi-user aggregation): registered second gmail.com user 'Ryan Chen', their initial GET shows sleep.user_rating=null (correct cross-user isolation); user2 POSTs {sleep, 1} → 200; sleep.count incremented by exactly 1 (2→3 in this run); user1 GET shows sleep.count=3 with user_rating still 5 (their own, NOT affected by user2's vote). Average correctly recomputed (3.33 in this run with 3 raters; would be 3.0 in clean DB with only the two test users — math is correct). Scenario E (validation): all 5 invalid bodies returned 400 with descriptive details — {stars:0}→400 'must be between 1 and 5', {stars:6}→400 same, {stars:'abc'}→400 'must be an integer 1..5', {app_id:'invalid_app',stars:3}→400 'Invalid app_id. Must be one of [...]', {missing stars}→400 'must be an integer 1..5'. Scenario F (anonymous via X-Anonymous-Id header): set header X-Anonymous-Id='anon-test-<uuid>' (no Authorization) → GET works (200 with all user_rating=null initially), POST {sleep, 2} → 200 saved, follow-up GET shows sleep.user_rating=2 persisted under that anon id, AND a different X-Anonymous-Id sees sleep.user_rating=null (correct isolation per anon device id). (3) Regression smoke (single test user with JWT): GET /api/profile → 200 with total_xp int; GET /api/stats/weekly → 200 with days length=7; GET /api/stats/monthly → 200 with days length=30; GET /api/stats/by-area → 200 with by_area:{social,fitness,appearance,mindset}; GET /api/library/catalog as admin (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com) → 200; GET /api/library/catalog as non-admin → 403. (4) Underlying collection name `library_ratings` and document shape (user_id, app_id, stars, updated_at, _id) verified indirectly via correctness — upsert semantics (Scenario B count unchanged) confirms the (user_id, app_id) composite uniqueness; cross-user aggregation (Scenario D) confirms global aggregation works; anonymous mode persistence under same X-Anonymous-Id confirms user_id is correctly derived from get_user_or_legacy. No bugs found."

  - task: "Monthly XP stats endpoint + restored stats/by-area + Focus Mode locked-app penalty (-15 XP/min)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "BATCH UPDATE. (1) Fixed two pre-existing bugs introduced in the prior session by the in-progress monthly endpoint commit: removed a duplicated `@api_router.get('/stats/weekly')` decorator, and added the missing `@api_router.get('/stats/by-area')` decorator that had been silently dropped. /api/stats/by-area was 404'ing before this fix — Progress tab Confidence Metric depended on it. (2) /api/stats/monthly is the new endpoint (already partially in place from prior session): returns 30-day window oldest→newest with same shape as /stats/weekly except `day` is the day-of-month abbreviation ('1', '15', '31') instead of weekday. (3) /api/focus/session now accepts new optional `locked_app_seconds` int field (Android UsageStatsManager-detected time inside blocklist apps). NEW XP rule: -15 XP per locked-MINUTE (rounded down), capped at -300, applies on BOTH completion AND early-exit (opening a blocked app is the violation regardless of whether the timer ran out). When locked_app_seconds is omitted (iOS / older builds), legacy fallback of -2 XP/min on backgrounded_seconds is preserved. focus_sessions DB rows now include locked_app_seconds for analytics. Need testing-agent run against /api/stats/monthly (shape, oldest→newest order, length=30) + /api/stats/by-area (after the regression fix, 200 with all 4 focus areas) + /api/focus/session with new field (penalty math: 4 min locked → -60, 0 locked → +bonus, 21 min locked → -300 cap, omitted → legacy -2/min)."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 69/69 assertions in /app/backend_test.py (run 2026-05-05 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Registered Maya Patel (gmail.com) — JWT returned immediately, 8 default tasks seeded. (1) GET /api/stats/monthly: 200, shape {days:[...]}, days length=30, days[0].date='2026-04-06' < days[29].date='2026-05-05' (oldest→newest), every day has {date, day, xp, gifted_xp, tasks}, days[0].day='6' (day-of-month string, NOT weekday like 'Mon'), days[29].date matches today UTC '2026-05-05'. After POST /tasks/{id}/complete on 'Morning reflection (5 min)' (15 XP): days[29].xp went from 0→15. (2) GET /api/stats/by-area: 200 (NOT 404 — restored decorator working), shape {by_area:{social:int, fitness:int, appearance:int, mindset:int}}. After completing a mindset default task ('Gratitude journal' 20 XP): by_area.mindset went 175→195 (delta=+20). (3) POST /api/focus/session — ALL 7 scenarios match spec exactly: A {25min, completed, locked=0} → xp_delta=+5, reason='focus_complete'; B {30min, actual=300s, locked=240s, NOT completed} → xp_delta=-60, reason='focus_distracted_locked_apps'; C {60min, completed, locked=120s} → xp_delta=-18 (+12 bonus -30 penalty), reason='focus_complete_with_penalty'; D {60min, completed, locked=1320s=22min} → xp_delta=-288 (+12 - capped 300), reason='focus_complete_with_penalty'; E {30min legacy iOS, omit locked_app_seconds, bg=120s} → xp_delta=-4 (2min×2), reason='focus_distracted'; F validation: planned=0 → 400, planned=200 → 400; G {25min cancel actual=30s, locked=0, bg=0} → xp_delta=0, reason='focus_cancelled_clean'. Profile total_xp tracking verified: starting=35 (from 2 task completions in (1)+(2)), then 35→40 (A) → 0 (B clamped, would be -20) → 0 (C clamped) → 0 (D clamped) → 0 (E) → 0 (G); /profile re-fetch matched session response after every scenario; total_xp NEVER went below 0. (4) Regression smoke: /auth/register (gmail.com) returns JWT for Ryan Chen; /profile via JWT → 200; /profile via X-Anonymous-Id → 200; /tasks GET → 200 with 8 seeded defaults; /stats/weekly → 200 with days length=7 and days[0].day='Wed' (weekday abbrev preserved). NOTE (minor, not blocking): /stats/by-area at server.py L1957 calls `db.task_logs.find({})` with NO user_id filter — it returns aggregated XP across ALL users. This explains why mindset starting value was 175 instead of 0 for a fresh user. The spec's assertion 'mindset increases after completing a mindset task' still passes because completing the user's task DOES increase the global sum. If user-scoped totals are intended, add `{'user_id': user_id}` to the query. No action required for this task per the review request."

Profile screen pills now render '08:00 MORNING · 13:00 AFTERNOON · 17:00 EVENING · 21:00 NIGHT' — visually verified live. (2) NEW _streak_warning_tick registered at 1-min interval alongside the other 3 jobs. At 20:00±5 min local time, for every profile with current_streak>0, counts task_logs rows for that user's local today (computed via day_start_time anchor matching server.user_today_str). If count==0, fires push '🔥 Your streak is in danger — complete one quest before midnight or lose your N-day streak!' with deeplink '/tasks'. Dedup'd per local-day via streak_warning_last_slot_key so we never double-ping. Scheduler now logs: 'started: motivation_tick + spot_surprise_tick + streak_warning_tick + match_invite_expiry'. (3) Focus Mode end notification — on session Start we register a dedicated HIGH-importance Android channel 'focus_complete' (green light, soft vibration) and schedule a local notification via Notifications.scheduleNotificationAsync with a seconds-based trigger equal to plannedMin*60. The notification id is stashed in endNotifIdRef and cancelled in finishSession() on early exit so we don't lie to the user 20 min later. Works offline + cross-process (expo-notifications stores the trigger natively). (4) Nav bar default = ALWAYS-SHOW. Flipped immersive.tsx default from `true` to `false` and inverted the AsyncStorage hydration: missing key = OFF (default), '1' = ON (opt-in auto-hide). Fresh installs now see the bottom tab bar pinned forever until the user explicitly toggles auto-hide ON. Screenshot live-verified toggle = ON with subtitle 'Bottom tab bar is pinned and never auto-hides'. (5) DM push notifications — confirmed already wired in /messages/send at line ~5235 so no new code needed there. (6) Spot surprise pushes (solo random + friends match broadcast) — confirmed already wired from the prior daylight scheduler commit (custom sound 'spot_surprise.wav' on Android 'spot_surprise' channel + iOS sound payload). Tests: live backend logs confirm all 4 scheduler jobs tick cleanly every 1 min / 20 sec without errors; _streak_warning_tick ran immediately after restart and completed in <3 ms (no matching users because no one's in the 20:00 window right now)."

    stuck_count: 0

    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "FEATURE — rewrote the previously fixed-5-min Focus Mode into a fully adjustable, gamified distraction-penalty session. FRONTEND (/app/frontend/app/focus.tsx): (1) Adjustable timer 1..180 min via ± chips OR 9 quick-select chips (5/10/15/20/25/30/45/60/90). Default 25 min (Pomodoro). (2) 'Commit to Avoiding' picker with 12 curated apps (YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, X, Facebook, Snapchat, Reddit, Discord, WhatsApp, Games, Netflix, Spotify) rendered as a 3-column tile grid with proper logo icons + platform tint. Default pre-committed: YouTube/Instagram/TikTok. (3) On Start, AppState listener tracks when app goes to background — at that moment we fire an expo-notifications HIGH-priority local notification ('🚨 Focus Mode breach — X min left — get back in and save your XP!') on a dedicated Android channel 'focus_breach' (MAX importance, lock-screen visibility, red vibration pattern). Accumulates `backgroundedSec`. (4) On foreground-return mid-session we show a Focus Wall modal: locked-app gallery with #777 greyed icons + blue LOCK_BLUE lock-badge on each tile + the running countdown. (5) Session-active taps on locked tiles trigger a 6px shake + warning haptic — 'can't open this now'. (6) On completion or End Early (with confirm dialog) we POST the session summary to /api/focus/session and show a Done screen with the XP delta (+5 for complete, −8 for 4min distracted, etc). BACKEND (/api/focus/session POST {planned_minutes, actual_seconds, backgrounded_seconds, completed, committed_app_count}): rule table — completed → +1 XP per 5 planned minutes (clamp 1..30); early exit with bg_min>0 → −2 XP per backgrounded minute (capped at −50 so one bad session can't nuke a user); clean cancel → 0 XP. Updates profile.total_xp + recomputes level, logs xp_events on +delta for leaderboard, writes a row to db.focus_sessions for history/anti-abuse. Smoke tested live at /tmp/focus_test.py — 5/5 assertions PASS (25-min complete=+5, 4-min bg=−8, clean cancel=0, 300-min rejected=400, 10-hour bg capped at −50)."
  - task: "Daylight-aware Spot scheduler (sunrise/sunset slots + multiplayer-broadcast night-skip + /admin/scheduler/daylight)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/notif_scheduler.py + /app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: true
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "ENHANCED Phase 1 — replaced fixed 09:00–21:00 surprise window with REAL sunrise/sunset per user. (1) Added `astral` (no API key) — `_user_daylight_today(prof)` resolves the profile timezone via a `_GEOCODE_OVERRIDES` map (29 common tz→city pairs) plus `tz.split('/')[-1]` fallback, then `astral.sun(loc.observer, date=local_today, tzinfo=tz)` — CRUCIALLY passing the LOCAL tz (NOT 'UTC' as I first did) because passing UTC interprets the calendar date as a UTC date and wraps the sunrise to the next day for tz offsets > 0 (the bug surfaced for Sydney where sunrise UTC came back as ~12 h after sunset UTC). The result is converted to UTC. Falls back to 06:00–20:00 local if geocode fails or sunset≤sunrise (high latitude). (2) `_generate_spot_slots_for_user(prof)` now picks 3 random slots inside (max(sunrise, fence_min), min(sunset, fence_max)), where fence is 08:00–21:00 local, with ≥90 min spacing. Slot output is sorted UTC iso strings. (3) `_is_daylight_now(prof)` — boolean gate consumed by /spot/match/create's broadcast loop: only friends whose CURRENT UTC time falls inside their own sunrise→sunset are pushed. Skipped friends are logged via `[match.invite.skip_night]` so the operator can see exactly why a friend didn't get the ping. The match itself is still created (host plays solo if needed). (4) New admin diagnostic GET /admin/scheduler/daylight?target_user_id= → returns `{user_id, timezone, now_utc, sunrise_utc, sunset_utc, is_daylight_now, next_3_random_slots_utc, stored_slots, stored_slots_day, stored_consumed}` for inspection. Live smoke-tested at /tmp/daylight_test.py with 8/8 assertions passing across THREE timezones (Sydney, Tokyo, LA): sunrise<sunset always; all 3 slots fall inside the daylight window; different tz → different sunrise UTC; 403 for non-admin; match-create still 200; broadcast log confirms `pushed=0 skipped_night=1` for a Tokyo friend who happened to be 27 min past sunset at test time → CORRECT behaviour. requirements.txt now pins astral==3.2."

(2) `_send_expo_push` now reads `data.channelId` so spot-surprise pushes route to the new Android channel + iOS uses `spot_surprise.wav`. (3) `/spot/match/create` now broadcasts a push notification to every confirmed friend with deeplink + 2-min `expires_at`. (4) Two new admin-only diagnostics: `GET /admin/scheduler/status` → `{running, jobs[{id,name,next_run_time,trigger}]}`; `POST /admin/scheduler/test-push {user_id?, kind:'motivation'|'spot_surprise'|'custom', title?, body?}` → fires a push immediately. Both 403 for non-admin. (5) Sound asset: Creator-supplied MP3 downloaded → converted to mono 22kHz WAV (132KB) at `/app/frontend/assets/sounds/spot_surprise.wav`, wired into expo-notifications plugin in app.json (`sounds: ['./assets/sounds/spot_surprise.wav']`) AND registered as the Android channel `spot_surprise` (HIGH importance, lock-screen visibility, vibration pattern) inside `PushTokenSync.tsx`. (6) `requirements.txt` updated: APScheduler==3.11.2, tzlocal==5.3.1. Smoke-tested live at https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api: 11/11 assertions pass (scheduler running with all 3 jobs, 403 gating, test-push returns sent counts, match create broadcast works, fresh match NOT cancelled by sweep, friend pairing flow works). Server-side scheduler logs confirm `[scheduler] started: motivation_tick + spot_surprise_tick + match_invite_expiry` and ticks running every 20s/60s without errors. NOTE: Phase 2 (frontend grace-period modal + Profile sync) deferred to next commit per scope budget."
  - task: "Boost activation — 500 fix on Creator-gifted custom boosts (POST /api/boosts/activate)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: true
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "BUG FIX (P0). User reported 'Internal Server Error' when activating a Creator-gifted Special Top-Up 2x boost. Root cause traced in two places: (1) `admin_gift_boost` writes inventory entries with `type='custom_gift'` for the custom path. The activation endpoint then did `cfg = BOOST_DEFS.get(boost_type)` followed by `cfg['duration_days']` — which crashes with `TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable` (→ 500) because 'custom_gift' is NOT a key in BOOST_DEFS. (2) Even for preset gifts (e.g. `double_week`), gifts override `duration_days=1` but the activation read 7 from BOOST_DEFS, silently giving the wrong window. FIX: rewrote `boosts_activate` to (a) prefer the inventory entry's own `multiplier` + `duration_days` + `label` (the gifting endpoint already populates these), with BOOST_DEFS as a last-ditch fallback only; (b) validate multiplier 2..10 and duration 1..365 before computing expires (rejects partially-written gifts with a clean 400 instead of 500); (c) add idempotent 409 if the entry is already activated (separate from the 'not in inventory' 404); (d) atomic update guard via `$elemMatch` on the SPECIFIC entry id + `activated:{$ne:true}` to prevent races (previous attempt used `boost_inventory.activated:$ne:true` which incorrectly treated the whole array atomically); (e) wrap the DB update in try/except so any unexpected motor error returns a friendly 500 with logger.exception traceback instead of bubbling raw. xp_boost doc now also stores duration_days + label for the timer card. Verified via /tmp/boost_fix_test.py against the live ingress (https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api): admin gifts CUSTOM (5x for 3 days) → recipient activates → 200 with multiplier=5, expires=+3d. Admin gifts PRESET (`double_week` overridden to 1 day) → activate → 200 with multiplier=2, expires=+1d (correctly honours gift override, not the 7d default). Idempotent re-activation → 409 (was 500). Bogus inventory_id → 404. Legacy {type:'triple_day'} path still 200."

          comment: "NEW: 'Dress with Confidence' AI photo coach. (1) POST /api/confidence/dress-advice {photo_base64?, message, event_context?, weather_hint?, history?} — uses GPT-4o-mini Vision via emergentintegrations + Emergent LLM key. Photo is downscaled to 640px JPEG q=80 for speed before being attached. System prompt enforces: start with one specific compliment, keep reply <120 words, factor weather + event into verdict, frame suggestions positively ('try swap X for Y', 'add Z to elevate'), end with one-line confidence booster. Returns {reply, entry_id}. After producing reply, persists a row to db.confidence_dress_history with {id, user_id, message, reply, event_context, weather_hint, thumbnail_base64 (320px b64 thumbnail of attached photo if any), has_photo, created_at}. (2) GET /api/confidence/dress-history?limit=30 (max 100, min 1) — returns {items:[{id, message, reply, event_context, weather_hint, thumbnail_base64, has_photo, created_at}, ...]} sorted newest first, scoped to user_id (depends on get_user_or_legacy → JWT or X-Anonymous-Id supported). (3) DELETE /api/confidence/dress-history/{entry_id} — returns {deleted:N}, scoped to caller's user_id (so cannot delete other users' entries). (4) GET /api/confidence/weather?lat=&lon= — thin httpx wrapper over open-meteo (no API key) returning {temperature_c, condition, precipitation_mm, wind_kmh, hint}. Entire feature is auth-aware: JWT or X-Anonymous-Id both work; missing creds fall back to legacy 'main' bucket. Vision call wrapped in try/except → 502 with friendly message on AI provider error."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 32/32 assertions in /app/dress_confidence_test.py (run 2026-04-30 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Registered A=Maya Patel and B=Ryan Chen on gmail.com — both got JWT immediately on /auth/register. (1) POST /confidence/dress-advice: (a) with synthetic 240×240 JPEG (PIL JPEG q=82) + message='Is this good for an office meeting?' + event_context='office' + weather_hint='15°C, light rain' → 200 with reply (308 chars, non-empty, reasonable size) + entry_id (uuid-shaped). (b) text-only with message='What should I wear to a date?' (no photo) → 200 with reply (413 chars) + entry_id. (c) Missing 'message' field → 422 Pydantic validation as expected (message is required). (d) Empty body {} → 422. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is configured in /app/backend/.env (sk-emergent-…), GPT-4o-mini Vision via emergentintegrations responded successfully — NO 503 'No LLM key configured' was hit. (2) GET /confidence/dress-history?limit=30 as A → 200 with items[] length=2, sorted newest-first by created_at, EVERY item has all 8 required keys {id, message, reply, event_context, weather_hint, thumbnail_base64, has_photo, created_at}. The photo entry has has_photo=True and thumbnail_base64 is a non-empty base64 string (5112 chars — the 320px q=70 thumbnail). The text-only entry has has_photo=False and thumbnail_base64 is null. limit=1 → exactly 1 item returned. User scoping verified: B's GET /confidence/dress-history → empty items:[] (no data leak across users). (3) DELETE /confidence/dress-history/{id}: (a) B (different user) trying to delete A's entry_id → 200 {deleted:0} (silent no-op, correctly scoped — NOT 404 as some APIs would do, exactly per spec). (b) Bogus entry_id 'bogus-<uuid>' → 200 {deleted:0}. (c) A (owner) deleting own entry_id → 200 {deleted:1}; subsequent GET /confidence/dress-history confirms the entry is gone. (4) GET /confidence/weather?lat=51.5&lon=0.1 (London) → 200 with all 5 required keys {temperature_c:16.8, condition:'unknown', precipitation_mm:0.0, wind_kmh:18.7, hint:'16.8°C, unknown, windy (18.7 km/h)'} and hint non-empty. NOTE: condition came back as 'unknown' for the live London call because open-meteo returned a weather_code not present in the code_map lookup table (weather_codes 4-44, 56-60, 66-70, 77-79, 83-94, 97-98 fall through to 'unknown'); not a bug per se — the spec said condition is one of 'clear/...rain/...' etc and 'unknown' is the documented fallback in the server code. Hint is still non-empty and well-formed. (b) lat=0,lon=0 (oceans) → 200 (open-meteo handles oceans). (5) Regression: GET /confidence/daily as A → 200 returning {date, social, physical, gratitude} with each track having a challenge object. POST /confidence/complete {track:'dress'} → 200 with ok=true (track='dress' is allowed via the Literal['social','physical','gratitude','dress'] in ConfidenceCompletePayload at L5707). POST /auth/login (correct creds) → 200 with fresh token. Admin login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) still healthy → 200. The full feature surface is working end-to-end with the live LLM."

(1) `_check_not_suspended(user_id)` is called inside `get_user_or_legacy` so EVERY authenticated request a suspended user makes returns HTTP 403 with detail={error:'account_suspended', message, until, forever, remaining_seconds, suspended_at, suspended_by, reason}. (2) `/auth/login` calls the same gate AFTER password verification so suspended users can't login. (3) POST /admin/suspend body={user_id, duration_hours?, forever?, reason?}. Creator-only (403 otherwise). 400 on self-suspend, 400 on attempting to suspend another admin, 400 on missing duration when forever=false, 400 on duration > 5 years, 404 on unknown user. Stores `suspended_until` (ISO timestamp or string 'forever'), `suspended_at`, `suspended_by_admin`, `suspension_reason` (≤280 chars). (4) POST /admin/unsuspend body={user_id} → unsets the four suspension fields; returns {ok, user_id, modified}. (5) GET /admin/suspension/{target_id} → admin-only — returns {user_id, suspended:bool, until?, forever?, remaining_seconds?, suspended_at?, suspended_by?, reason?}."

  - task: "Friends Weekly Leaderboard (timezone-scoped Mon-Sat window + Sunday winner + medal grant)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "New GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=<offset_minutes> endpoint. Each task completion in POST /api/tasks/{id}/complete now writes a doc to db.xp_events: {user_id, xp, earned_at_utc, tz_offset_minutes, local_week_key='YYYY-Www'}. Leaderboard logic: (a) members = viewer + accepted friends (de-duped). (b) For each member, compute THEIR OWN local Monday 00:00 → Sunday 00:00 window using their stored tz_offset_minutes (falls back to viewer's tz if null), then sum xp_events.xp in that [UTC-converted] range. (c) rows sorted by weekly_xp desc, each row includes: user_id, name, avatar, level, total_xp, weekly_xp, is_self, tz_offset_minutes, is_week_closed (viewer is in local Sunday), medals_count, medals_revoked. (d) If viewer's local-now weekday == Sunday and top row weekly_xp > 0 → winner is declared for the week that just ended; if no majority-supported active report against them, db.leaderboard_medals receives a {user_id, week_key, xp, revoked:false} doc and winner's boost_inventory gets a 'double_day' 2× XP-for-a-day entry appended (idempotent by (user_id, week_key)). If majority cheating-report exists, medal is inserted with revoked=true and NO bonus is granted. Please verify: (1) fresh users show weekly_xp=0; after completing a task the event is logged and sum increases; (2) tz param is persisted to viewer's profile; (3) two users in different tz each get their own Mon-Sat window; (4) winner is NOT declared Mon-Sat (viewer_is_sunday=false); (5) on Sunday, winner auto-gets medal + double_day entry in inventory (check status); (6) re-calling leaderboard on same Sunday doesn't duplicate the medal or boost."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 13/13 assertions. Fresh anonymous user GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=0 → 200 with rows=[1 self row {weekly_xp:0, is_self:true}], reports:[], viewer_is_sunday boolean, week_key 'YYYY-Www'. tz=330 call persists tz_offset_minutes=330 to profile (verified via GET /profile). Completed first default task (xp_value=15) → POST /tasks/{id}/complete returned xp_awarded=15 (no boost). Re-fetched leaderboard → self_row.weekly_xp=15 = awarded XP, confirming xp_events doc was inserted and summation across the local Mon-Sat window works. Idempotency: two consecutive same-day calls return identical medals_count (Mon-Sat → no winner logic triggered, viewer_is_sunday=false today). Sort-order check (rows sorted desc by weekly_xp) verified separately on the 2-member leaderboard in the report-system test below — passes. Sunday-winner medal-grant + double_day inventory push could not be exercised directly since today is Mon-Sat in UTC, but the underlying _grant_winner_medal is keyed idempotently by (user_id, week_key) and re-calls return the existing medal doc by design.     implemented: true

          comment: "New endpoints: (1) POST /api/leaderboard/report {reported_user_id, reason} → must be on your leaderboard (friend or self), inserts db.leaderboard_reports doc keyed by week_key; reporter auto-added to supporters[]; 400 on self-report or duplicate same-week report. (2) POST /api/leaderboard/report/{id}/support → adds user_id to supporters. (3) DELETE /api/leaderboard/report/{id}/support → removes user_id. (4) Reports surface in GET /api/friends/leaderboard → reports[] array shows active reports visible to viewer with {id, reporter_name, reported_name, reason, week_key, supporters_count, viewer_supported, viewer_is_reporter}. Revocation logic: when viewer is on local Sunday and top-of-leaderboard winner has been reported, we count unique supporters who are ALSO on the leaderboard (viewer+friends). If supporters_count >= floor(N/2)+1 (strict majority) → _winner_report_verdict returns guilty=true → medal is inserted with revoked=true + revoked_reason and NO bonus is granted. Test: (1) report-then-support flow returns expected supporters counts; (2) majority triggers guilty verdict when ≥floor(N/2)+1 supporters on a N-member leaderboard; (3) duplicate report by same reporter in same week → 400; (4) reporting a non-leaderboard member → 400; (5) self-report → 400."
